text.title: llm-friendly-architecture Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for llm-friendly-architecture as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: God component: single file over 800 lines combining routing, auth, data fetching, state, calculations, and rendering.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Context truncation causes the agent to miss the lower half of the file. Multiple concerns mean any change risks unintended side effects.

failure-mode.description: Utils dumping ground: utils/index.ts with 50+ unrelated functions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents cannot infer which function to edit without reading the entire file. New functions get added to the dump rather than purpose-named files.

failure-mode.description: Barrel re-exports: components/index.ts with export * from './Button' for 20+ components.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents edit the barrel as if it is the implementation. Circular dependency risk increases. TypeScript path alias cache may mask broken imports after restructuring.

failure-mode.description: Inline large data: 200+ lines of JSON or data arrays inside a component function body.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inflates the file past the point where the agent holds the full rendering logic in context. Data line edits corrupt adjacent rendering code.

failure-mode.description: Ambiguous file names: helpers.ts, common.ts, shared.ts, misc.ts as catch-all files.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents search by filename. Multiple ambiguous candidates cause wrong-file edits that produce no-ops or bugs in unrelated modules.
